tasks.description: Phase 1, Step 1: Rewrite createShotWithGenerationPath (singular) to use create_shot_with_generations RPC N=1. Change CreateShotWithGenerationPathInput in shotCreationTypes.ts to replace createShotWithImage callback with createShotWithGenerations, remove generationPreview/shots fields. Rewrite createShotWithGenerationPath in shotCreationPaths.ts to call createShotWithGenerations({projectId, shotName, generationIds:[generationId]}), reuse mapRpcShotGenerationToRow/buildShotStats, call upsertShotInCache, enqueueGenerationsInvalidation(includeShots:false), invalidateShotsQueries(refetchType:'inactive'). Remove applyAtomicShotCacheUpdate import. Update CreateShotActionInput to remove createShotWithImageMutation. Update dispatcher in shotCreationAction.ts so singular path passes createShotWithGenerationsMutation. Update useShotCreation.ts hook to remove useCreateShotWithImage import/instantiation.

Phase 1, Step 2: Update all four test files affected by singular path migration. (1) shotCreationPaths.test.ts — rewrite singular test to mock createShotWithGenerations, assert upsertShotInCache called (not applyAtomicShotCacheUpdate), assert enqueueGenerationsInvalidation with includeShots:false. (2) shotCreationAction.test.ts — remove createShotWithImageMutation from all renderHook calls, update assertion at line ~110 to expect createShotWithGenerations. (3) __tests__/useShotCreation.test.ts — remove useCreateShotWithImage mock. (4) shots/__tests__/useShotCreation.test.ts — delete describe('useCreateShotWithImage') block, keep useHandleExternalImageDrop tests.

Phase 3, Step 5: Create duplicate_as_new_generation database function. Create migration file supabase/migrations/20260406_duplicate_as_new_generation.sql. RPC takes p_shot_id, p_generation_id, p_project_id, p_timeline_frame, p_next_timeline_frame, p_target_timeline_frame. Body: fetch primary variant for location/thumbnail/params, insert new generation with based_on and params.source:'timeline_duplicate', insert primary variant link, fetch existing timeline frames, calculate target frame with collision resolution, insert shot_generation, return JSONB with new_generation_id, new_shot_generation_id, timeline_frame, location, thumbnail_url, type, params, created_at. Check existing migration naming convention in supabase/migrations/ first.

Phase 3, Step 6: Rewrite useDuplicateAsNewGeneration to use the atomic RPC. Replace duplicateAsNewGeneration function (lines ~211-244) with single RPC call. Simplify onSuccess: remove 5 broad invalidateQueries calls, replace with enqueueGenerationsInvalidation(scope:'all', includeShots:false, projectId, includeProjectUnified:true) + invalidateShotsQueries(refetchType:'inactive'). MUST PRESERVE queryKeys.generations.derivedGenerations(original_id) and queryKeys.segments.parents(shot_id, project_id) invalidation. Remove helper functions: fetchSourceGenerationData, createDuplicatedGeneration, insertPrimaryVariant, fetchExistingTimelineFrames, insertShotGeneration, resolveTimelineCollision, buildTargetTimelineFrame.

Phase 3, Step 7: Update/create tests for duplicate-as-new-generation. Rewrite test for the hook — mock the RPC call, assert single invocation. Assert enqueueGenerationsInvalidation called with correct options. Assert queryKeys.generations.derivedGenerations and queryKeys.segments.parents are still invalidated.

Phase 2, Step 3: Add post-upload cache hydration to createShotWithFilesPath. Add queryClient to CreateShotWithFilesPathInput in shotCreationTypes.ts. After uploadToShot returns in shotCreationPaths.ts, query shot_generations joined with generations for the new shot (select id, generation_id, timeline_frame, generations!inner(location, thumbnail_url, type, created_at, starred, name, based_on, params, primary_variant_id) where shot_id = newShotId). Map to GenerationRow[] using mapRpcShotGenerationToRow or lightweight adapter for nested shape. Build full Shot via buildShotStats. Call upsertShotInCache. Add standard invalidation pattern: enqueueGenerationsInvalidation(includeShots:false, includeProjectUnified:true) + invalidateShotsQueries(refetchType:'inactive'). Thread queryClient through from shotCreationAction.ts.

Phase 2, Step 4: Update tests for file upload path. Update shotCreationPaths.test.ts file upload test to pass queryClient in input. Mock the Supabase from().select().eq() call for post-upload fetch. Assert upsertShotInCache called with complete shot data. Assert enqueueGenerationsInvalidation and invalidateShotsQueries called.

Phase 4, Step 8: Delete applyAtomicShotCacheUpdate and its test. Delete src/shared/hooks/shotCreation/shotCacheUpdate.ts. Delete src/shared/hooks/shotCreation/shotCacheUpdate.test.ts. Grep to verify no remaining imports reference these files.

Phase 4, Step 9: Delete useCreateShotWithImage mutation from src/shared/hooks/shots/useShotCreation.ts (lines ~24-83). Remove export from src/shared/hooks/shots/index.ts (line ~30). Remove enqueueGenerationsInvalidation import if no longer used in the file. Verify no remaining callers via grep.

Phase 4, Step 10: Audit enqueueGenerationsInvalidation callers. Grep for includeShots, delayMs, setTimeout.*invalidat across shot creation code. Verify no stale patterns remain after T1-T9. The audit should confirm: (1) includeShots:true callers outside creation flows (useShotGenerationMutations, useTimelineCore, ShotEditorView, useDeleteActions) are intentional and unchanged. (2) No delayMs usage remains in shot creation onSuccess handlers. (3) No setTimeout-based invalidation hacks remain.

Phase 4, Step 11: Run all tests and verify. Run: npx vitest run src/shared/hooks/shotCreation/ && npx vitest run src/shared/hooks/shots/ && npx vitest run src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useShotCreation.test.ts && npx vitest run src/shared/hooks/invalidation/. Then run full test suite. If any test fails, read the error, fix the code, and re-run until all pass. Also grep codebase for isOptimistic to confirm no optimistic cache entries are created in shot creation paths. Grep for applyAtomicShotCacheUpdate and useCreateShotWithImage to confirm full removal.

watch_items: FLAG-001: useDuplicateAsNewGeneration.onSuccess MUST preserve segments.parents and generations.derivedGenerations invalidation — enqueueGenerationsInvalidation does NOT cover these
FLAG-002: createShotWithFilesPath post-upload fetch uses nested join shape (generations!inner(...)) not flat RPC shape — needs adapter when mapping to GenerationRow[]
FLAG-003: Four test files must all be updated — shotCreationPaths.test.ts, shotCreationAction.test.ts, __tests__/useShotCreation.test.ts, shots/__tests__/useShotCreation.test.ts
Debt watch: Do not introduce any new setTimeout-based invalidation hacks
Debt watch: Do not add isOptimistic:true entries to any cache update path
Assumption: create_shot_with_generations RPC handles N=1 correctly (FOREACH over single-element array)
Assumption: useHandleExternalImageDrop per-file optimistic updates via addImageToShot continue working during upload — post-upload upsertShotInCache replaces partial entries with real data
Assumption: deprecated createShot field on CreateShotWithGenerationsPathInput was already removed in previous megaplan
Risk: The singular path rewrite (T1) may be nearly identical to the plural path — consider calling plural path directly rather than duplicating code
Risk: Migration file naming must match existing convention in supabase/migrations/ — check before creating
Risk: Collision resolution logic in SQL RPC (T3) is non-trivial — test edge cases (adjacent frames, no gap, max offset 1000)

sense_checks.question: After rewrite, does createShotWithGenerationPath follow the exact 5-step pattern: RPC → map to Shot → upsertShotInCache → enqueueGenerationsInvalidation(includeShots:false) → invalidateShotsQueries(inactive)?
Are all four test files updated and do they no longer reference createShotWithImage, applyAtomicShotCacheUpdate, or createShotWithImageMutation?
Does the SQL RPC handle collision resolution atomically (midpoint calculation, frame offset up to 1000) and return all fields needed to build a GenerationRow?
Does the rewritten onSuccess preserve BOTH queryKeys.segments.parents(shot_id, project_id) AND queryKeys.generations.derivedGenerations(original_id) invalidation alongside the standard pattern?
Do tests assert that segments.parents and generations.derivedGenerations invalidation are called, not just enqueueGenerationsInvalidation?
Does the post-upload fetch correctly handle the nested join shape (generations!inner) and map it to GenerationRow[] compatible with upsertShotInCache?
Does the test mock the full Supabase chain (from→select→eq) and assert upsertShotInCache receives a complete Shot with correct generation data?
After deletion, does grep confirm zero remaining imports of applyAtomicShotCacheUpdate or shotCacheUpdate anywhere in the codebase?
After deletion, does grep confirm zero remaining imports of useCreateShotWithImage, and is the export removed from shots/index.ts?
Is the audit complete — no delayMs in creation handlers, no setTimeout invalidation hacks, and all remaining includeShots:true callers are legitimate non-creation flows?
Do all targeted test suites and the full test suite pass? Are grep checks for isOptimistic, applyAtomicShotCacheUpdate, and useCreateShotWithImage clean?

meta_commentary:
Execution order: T1+T3 can run in parallel (Phase 1 code + Phase 3 RPC are independent). T2 follows T1, T4 follows T3, T5 follows T4, T6 follows T1 (needs singular path done first to avoid merge conflicts in shared files). T7 follows T6. T8+T9 follow their dependencies. T10 is the final audit. T11 is the verification gate.

Key judgment calls for the executor:
1. For T1, the singular path will look nearly identical to the plural path in createShotWithGenerationsPath. Consider whether to extract shared logic or just call the plural path directly with generationIds:[generationId]. The plan suggests both options — pick whichever produces less code.
2. For T3, the collision resolution logic currently lives in useDuplicateAsNewGeneration.ts as buildTargetTimelineFrame and resolveTimelineCollision. Replicate this faithfully in SQL — don't simplify or change the algorithm. The midpoint calculation and +30 offset with retry up to 1000 must match.
3. For T6, the Supabase join shape returns {id, generation_id, timeline_frame, generations: {location, ...}} whereas the RPC mapper expects flat rows. Write a small adapter — don't try to reuse mapRpcShotGenerationToRow directly if the shape doesn't match.
4. The plan's execution order says Phase 3 before Phase 2, which is reflected here (T3-T5 before T6-T7). This is intentional — the duplicate RPC is independent and the file upload path is trickier.
5. Deploy the migration (T3) with `npx supabase db push --linked` — do NOT use `db reset --linked` per CLAUDE.md safety rules.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Rewrite createShotWithGenerationPath to use create_shot_with_generations RPC N=1
Step 2: Update all four test files affected by singular path migration
Step 3: Add post-upload cache hydration to createShotWithFilesPath
Step 4: Update tests for file upload path
Step 6: Rewrite useDuplicateAsNewGeneration to use atomic RPC
Step 8: Delete applyAtomicShotCacheUpdate and its test
Step 11: Run all tests and verify

validation.completeness_notes: All 11 plan steps mapped 1:1 to tasks. Execution order follows the plan's recommended phasing: Phase 1 (T1-T2) and Phase 3 (T3-T5) can proceed in parallel, Phase 2 (T6-T7) after Phase 1, Phase 4 cleanup (T8-T10) after all migrations, final verification (T11) last.
